title: 5 Ways League of Dungeoneers Is Different Than Warhammer Quest 95; and 10 Tips for Survival!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kO5VQOB3sxI

Daniel (@dungeondive) draws five key distinctions between League of Dungeoneers and Warhammer Quest 1995, then shares ten designer-sourced survival tips. The differences: (1) Character creation is far deeper—combining species, profession, stat rolls, skills, talents, and backgrounds in an hour-long session zero. (2) Combat is tactical rather than hack-and-slash, requiring attention to facing, spacing, dodging, and defensive stances. (3) The world is more generically fantasy, making it easier to bolt on third-party supplements like Loke Battle Mats' random encounters. (4) Linear campaigns coexist with the survival-campaign structure. (5) Survival gear management is crucial—empty bottles for your alchemist are cause for celebration.

The ten tips from designer Michael: buy armour or bandages immediately; expect heroes to get knocked out; buy rations for short rests (which also lower dungeon threat); avoid poking sarcophagi; build a balanced party with a lockpicker; learn when to defend; prioritise stun weapons; close doors behind you; don't bash doors unnecessarily (noise attracts wanderers); and always bring a light source for fear/terror tests.

----

Does League of Dungeoneers fill the Warhammer Quest-shaped void, or is it its own beast entirely?
